title: PUBLIC SAFETY POWER SHUTOFFS - 211info
text: SALEM ELECTRIC PUBLIC SAFETY POWER SHUTOFFS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A Public Safety Power Shutoff (PSPS) is an operational practice an electric utility may use to shut off power in high-risk areas to help prevent wildfires during extreme and dangerous weather conditions. PSPS means Salem Electric will shut off power, and it is considered an action of last resort to help protect public safety. 633 7th Street NW Salem, OR 97304 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 362-3601 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (503) 362-3601 Main phone Main phone (503) 362-3601 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Visit website for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None AREA SERVED: Cities of Keizer and Salem TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Cherriots bus stops within a block of the office. Services: Electric Service Connection/Repair * Disaster/Emergency Issues Other Services Offered At This Location: HOUSING/SHELTER - WEATHERIZATION PROGRAM UTILITIES - SALEM ELECTRIC MEMBER ASSISTANCE PROGRAM If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. SALEM ELECTRIC
